name: Insecure Policy Set via Set-ExecutionPolicy
description: Detects the use of Set-ExecutionPolicy to set insecure policies such as Unrestricted, Bypass, or RemoteSigned.

prompts.prompt: High-severity alert: An insecure PowerShell execution policy was set on a Windows system. This action, often employed by attackers, significantly lowers the system's security posture by allowing the execution of unsigned or remote scripts, potentially enabling malicious code execution. Event Details: **Timestamp** {timestamp}, **Process GUID** {process_guid}, **Process Path** {process_path}, **Command Line** {process_command_line}, **User** {user}. Immediately investigate to determine the legitimacy of this policy change. If unauthorized, revert the policy to a secure state and scan the system for signs of compromise.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the PowerShell script block logging event.

parameters.name: process_guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the process that set the execution policy.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the PowerShell executable or host process.

parameters.name: process_command_line
parameters.description: The full command line that includes 'Set-ExecutionPolicy' and an insecure policy like 'Unrestricted', 'Bypass', or 'RemoteSigned'.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that executed the PowerShell command.
